note: Pins the StyleSettings colour-slot chain that paints Writer's ruler under the Material file-widget theme. Writer's ruler is SwCommentRuler (a compiled SvxRuler subclass) whose Paint() delegates to the shared Ruler::ImplDraw in svtools/source/control/ruler.cxx, plus SwCommentRuler's own DrawCommentControl. Every colour those functions read from StyleSettings (GetWindowColor/GetDialogColor/GetFaceColor/GetLightColor/GetShadowColor/GetDarkShadowColor/GetWorkspaceColor for the page-track/margin/border/indent geometry; GetHighlightColor/GetHighlightTextColor/GetButtonTextColor for the Writer Comments toggle) is populated from the definition.xml <style> slots by FileDefinitionWidgetDraw::updateSettings when the Material theme is selected. This is a source-composition pin over unmodified upstream files: runtime_verified is false throughout -- no native build, ruler pixels, or runtime interaction are claimed. svtools/source/control/ruler.cxx is a SHARED control (other applications' rulers use it too); the claim is scoped to the shared Ruler paint path as consumed by Writer's SwCommentRuler, not Writer-exclusive native ruler work that does not exist.

style_slots.role: border-marker dark edge + drag-indent triangle hit
drag-indent triangle idle + collapsed comment-toggle fill

bridge.note: updateSettings copies every definition.xml <style> colour into the live StyleSettings; a dropped or renamed assignment breaks the token chain the ruler reads and fails closed.

paint_paths.note: SwCommentRuler::Paint delegates to SvxRuler::Paint (the shared base) then paints the Writer Comments toggle via DrawCommentControl.

The shared Ruler::ImplDraw page-track/margin/border/indent painting that SwCommentRuler inherits unchanged. The default (Material) page-track and margin fills are the else branch of the ThemeColors carve-out.

carveout.name: office-theme-colors
carveout.note: When the user opts into LibreOffice's separate office-theme-colors feature, Ruler::ImplDraw takes ThemeColors::GetThemeColors() for the page-track/margin fill instead of the Material StyleSettings slots. officecfg defaults it Disabled, so the Material pinning holds by default; this branch is recorded spec-only and is NEVER claimed as covered by the Material token chain. The branch_marker must remain present in source so the carve-out reflects real code.
